data.title: Boston Sci gets FDA okay for Farapulse cardiac mapping products
data.description:
• Boston Scientific (NYSE:BSX) said it has received FDA approval for its Farawave NAV Ablation Catheter for the treatment of paroxysmal atrial fibrillation and Faraview software.
  • The products are used to provide visualization during pulsed field ablation, or PFA, procedures with the company's Farapulse PFA system.
  • The technologies are compatible with Boston Scientific’s existing cardiac mapping technology and its Opal HDx Mapping System. according to a statement.
  • Boston Scientific received FDA clearance for its Farapulse PFA System for the treatment of atrial fibrillation in January.

data.title: Humana gains amid reports of renewed Cigna talks (update)
data.description:
Update 4:40pm: Adds Bloomberg reporting.

Humana (NYSE:HUM) gained 3% in after hours trading amid reports that it's resumed talks with rival Cigna (CI). Cigna fell 1.5%.

The two managed care providers had had informal talks recently about a potential deal, according to a Bloomberg report on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter. Earlier Friday, there was a Betaville "uncooked" that there was speculation that Humana received a takeover bid. Cigna and a Western European company may be interested, according to the report.

No decision has been made and Cigna or Humana may push any deal past the new year or could decide to abandon any deal, according to Bloomberg.

Cigna (CI) is trying to close the sale of its Medicare Advantage business in the coming weeks before going forward with another deal, Bloomberg reported.

Humana (HUM) declined to comment to Seeking Alpha, saying it has a policy of not discussing market rumors or speculation.

The deal talk comes after Cigna (CI) and Humana (HUM) last December scrapped plans to merge after being unable to agree on financial terms, including price. Investors failed to embrace the idea of a merger, with Cigna shares dropping 8% following news of an impending deal.

The WSJ said at the time that the proposed merger deal would have had Cigna acquire Humana in a cash-and-stock deal with a “large” stock component. There was also some speculation last October about Walmart (WMT) and Humana (HUM) possibly reviving talks that were reported in 2018. The WSJ reported in March 2018 that Walmart was in preliminary talks to buy Humana.

A potential Humana (HUM) sale comes after the managed care company's shares have plunged 41% this year. Humana (HUM) could be more vulnerable to a takeover after its shares sunk 22% on the first two days of the month after the company announced a sharp decline in membership enrolled in its top-rated Medicare Advantage (MA) plans, its latest headwind related to government-backed insurance.

Humana (HUM) is set to report Q3 results on Oct. 30.

data.title: MultiMetaVerse receives non-compliance notice from Nasdaq
data.description:
• MultiMetaVerse Holdings (NASDAQ:MMV) received a letter from Nasdaq stating that the company was not in compliance with Nasdaq Listing Rule.
  • The company has until October 22, 2024 to request a hearing before the Nasdaq Hearings Panel.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Ynvisible announces non-brokered private placement
data.description:
• Ynvisible Interactive (TSXV:YNV:CA) on Friday said it has arranged a non-brokered private placement financing of up to 5M common shares at a price of $0.10 per share, for aggregate gross proceeds of up to $500,000.
  • Proceeds from the private placement will be used as working capital and for general corporate purposes.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Ryan Kalt acquires 1M common shares of Dixie Gold
data.description:
• Dixie Gold (TSXV:DG:CA) said on Friday that Ryan Kalt has acquired 1M common share units of the company on a direct basis through a private placement.
  • Acquired Units were acquired by Kalt on a direct basis for investment purposes, with cash consideration of $50,000 paid to the Issuer.
  • Kalt now owns and controls, on a direct and indirect basis a total of 11.7M common shares of Dixie Gold, representing approximately 36.81% of the Issuer’s currently issued and outstanding common shares.

data.title: Google wins delay of order to open up app store from Epic case
data.description:
Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) has won a temporary delay of a directive to open up its app store while a federal court rules on the company's appeal.

U.S. District Judge James Donato allowed for the delay of his order for Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) to allow for developers to set up their own app marketplaces independent of its Play Store, and to do so by Nov. 1 -- a deadline Google bristled at, suggesting it wasn't technically possible.

The Oct. 7 order stemmed from Google's loss in a lawsuit from Epic Games, developer of hit videogame Fortnite, after years of challenges from Epic to app-store leaders Google and Apple (AAPL).

The Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals is set to look at the handling of the trial, which Google lost last December.

Epic Games is majority owned by its CEO Tim Sweeney, though Tencent (OTCPK:TCEHY) maintains a 35% stake and Sony (SONY) a 5.4% holding.

See what's at stake in the recent ruling over allowing alternative app stores.

data.title: Euda Health files for $100M mixed securities shelf
data.description:
• Euda Health (NASDAQ:EUDA) filed a prospectus related to the offer and sale of $100M mixed securities shelf.
  • This preliminary prospectus is not an offer to sell these securities.
  • Filing

data.title: Public Storage appoints Chris Sambar as COO
data.description:
• Public Storage (NYSE:PSA) on Friday has appointed Chris Sambar as chief operating officer.
  • Most recently, Sambar was president at AT&T Communications. He also served in various leadership positions, including sales, operations, and corporate strategy, since joining AT&T in 2002.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Ontrak files offering of common stock
data.description:
• Ontrak (NASDAQ:OTRK) filed preliminary prospectus for offering up to 6,449,481 shares of our common stock issuable upon the exercise of outstanding warrants.
  • SEC Filing

data.title: Flux Power receives non-compliance letter from Nasdaq
data.description:
• Flux Power (NASDAQ:FLUX) said on Friday that it has received a letter from Nasdaq, notifying the Company that it was not in compliance with requirements of Nasdaq’s listing rules as a result of not having timely filed its Annual Report with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
  • The company said it is working to complete its Form 10-K and plans to file its Form 10-K as promptly as practicable to regain compliance with the Listing Rule.
